case_title: ALICIA MARSHALL VS. SAN FRANCISCO FORD

docket_entries.description: MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $7,500.00 FILED BY MARSHALL, ALICIA AS TO DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORD ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR AUG-02-2010 AT 06:30 PM IN DEPT 506

PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVED JUN-30-2010, COURT CERTIFIED MAIL RETURNED SERVED AS TO DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORD
REQUEST TO POSTPONE SMALL CLAIMS HEARING FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORD
CLAIM OF DEFENDANT FOR $1,023.03 FILED BY SAN FRANCISCO FORD AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT MARSHALL, ALICIA ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR AUG-02-2010 AT 06:30 PM IN DEPT 506
ORDER ON REQUEST TO POSTPONE THE SMALL CLAIMS HEARING. THE REQUEST IS GRANTED. -COMMISSIONER PAUL SLAVIT-
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF AUG-02-2010 - CONTINUED PER REQUEST OF DEFENDANT TO SEP-03-2010 AT 3:30 PM IN DEPT. 506(PLAINTIFIFF'S CLAIM)

DECLARATION OF DUE DILIGENCE. FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORD
REQUEST TO POSTPONE SMALL CLAIMS HEARING FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORD
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF SEP-03-2010 - CONTINUED PER REQUEST OF DEFENDANT TO OCT-13-2010 AT 3:30 PM IN DEPT. 506 (DEFENDANT'S CLAIM)

ORDER ON REQUEST TO POSTPONE THE SMALL CLAIMS HEARING. THE REQUEST IS GRANTED.THE COURT GRANTS LEAVE FOR THIS LATE REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE. IF DEFENDANT'S CLAIM HAS NOT BEEN SERVED BEFORE CONTINUED HEARING DATE, THE CASE WILL PROCEED TO TRIAL ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. -COMMISSIONER PAUL SLAVIT-
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF SEP-03-2010 - CONTINUED PER REQUEST OF DEFENDANT TO OCT-13-2010 AT 3:30 PM IN DEPT. 506
SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF OCT-13-2010 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF OCT-13-2010 BY PAUL SLAVIT. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF ONLY. PLAINTIFF EXHIBITS RETAINED. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506)
SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF OCT-13-2010 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF OCT-13-2010 BY PAUL SLAVIT. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF ONLY. PLAINTIFF EXHIBITS RETAINED. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506)

DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF WAS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY FOR THE CAR FIRE. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A NEW ACTION, SUPPORTED BY ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE.

DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF WAS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY FOR THE CAR FIRE. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A NEW ACTION, SUPPORTED BY ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE.
EXHIBITS RETURNED TO PLAINTIFF MARSHALL, ALICIA
REMARK: FILE IS DESTROYED PURSUANT TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 68153. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED ON MAY-26-2026.
